statement.markdown: You're given an array of integers and a target integer. Your function should return the indices of the two numbers in the array that add up to the target. If there are multiple solutions, return the first pair of indices you find. If no such pair exists, return an empty array.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be an array of integers and a target integer. e.g. [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], 7
statement.outputFormat: Return an array of two indices. e.g. [1, 3]

hints.text: Think about how you could use a hash table to solve this problem efficiently.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: The idea is to create a hash table where the keys are the values in the array and the values are the indices of those values.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach would be to iterate over the array and for each element, iterate over the rest of the array to find a pair that adds up to the target.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a hash table to store the values and their indices. Then, for each element in the array, check if the difference between the target and the current element is in the hash table. If it is, return the indices.
commonMistakes: Not considering the case where the pair is the same number.
Not handling the case where the target is not found.
